title: Persistent Headaches
body: Worsening headaches that don't respond to typical pain medication — often most severe in the morning or after lying down.

title: Vision Changes
body: Blurred or double vision, loss of peripheral vision, or other unexplained visual disturbances.

title: Hearing Loss
body: Progressive or sudden hearing loss, tinnitus (ringing in the ears), or a feeling of fullness in the ears.

title: Memory & Cognitive Issues
body: Difficulty concentrating, memory lapses, confusion, or noticeable changes in thinking ability.

title: Seizures
body: New-onset seizures with no prior history, or changes in existing seizure patterns.

title: Weakness or Numbness
body: Weakness, numbness, or tingling in the arms or legs — particularly on one side of the body.
